feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Mass update Purchasing Group in Components
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to mass update purchasing group in components of work order. Through LSMW can we do through Direct Input Method?

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Hands-On Practice Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management Exercises and Solutions Manual.
entry.content: I went through the Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management Exercises and Solutions Manual. In the document file, it shows there are 75 pages upon launching, but the last page ends at page 69. I think the document file is missing some pages. Can someone confirm this? ￼

entry.title: HOW CONFIGURE WORKFLOW ON MAINTENANCE ORDERS S4 PUBLIC CLOUD?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,
We are participating in a project for S4hana Public Cloud and are encountering some difficulties configuring the maintenance order workflows. They are created, have been added to teams and users, but the orders simply don't arrive for approval in the Inbox app (My Inbox). The maintenance orders have a "send for approval" status, and we cannot approve them anywhere. Do you know of any missing configuration, or are they approved in other apps and not in My Inbox? We are using version 2602.

Thank you in advance,

Adilson

entry.title: Unable to modify Plant field in components tab for maintenance order after migration to S4H
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Before migrating to S4HANA we were able to modify Plant field in components tab for maintenance orders, as seen below the field is not greyed out.

However, after migrating to S4H, we are now unable to modify this field.

I have checked customizing 

And the field seems to be OK

Plus, BADI IWO1_SCREEN_MODIFY does not seem to have been implemented according to SE19

Not sure about what to do, any thoughts on how to proceed to go back to normal?

Thanks in advance,

Sebastian

entry.title: Find Technical Object- Manage Structure button is disabled
entry.content:
In sap pm public cloud, Find Technical Object Application, Manage Structure button is disabled.

Kindly help us to enable "Manage Structure" button.

entry.title: Plant maintenance BOM structure (product structure) in Power BI
entry.content:
The purpose of the report is data cleansing and data maintenance. This is the structure we have. MARA (Material) => direct Eq or FL BOM; MARA (Material) => material BOM (type L or I) => Equipment/FL. Construction type is used in some cases but in most of the cases both material BOM and construction type are used, which causes duplication. Taken steps: I linked MARA => STPO=> STKO=>Bridge Technical objects. Bridge Technical objects consists of MAST + EQST + TPST, so FLs, equipment and material BOMs are all Technical objects. So, currently I have the following structure that works. MM => direct link to EQ, FL or Material BOM. The only source I have is SE16N. Now I want to link material BOMs to Eq or FL when applicable but I don't know how to proceed. The link MAST => Equipment and FL I am missing. 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Read the order long text before save in IW31/creation
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Could you please help me in achieving the below requirement?

Need to read the SAP PM order long text before save i.e when I am creating a new order via IW31 and saving it. During this time the order number is not generated and also the text id. We used Read_text and Get_text_memory FM but not getting anything. Read_text works perfectly if the order is in change mode i.e the order has been already saved one time. 

Step by step guide would be rewarding.

Thanks

entry.title: LAM (Linear Asset Management) - remove markers from with references to "where used list"
entry.content:
Hello,

we send LAM data from our GIS system to SAP. I maintain the GIS and the LAM data in the GIS — I am not very familiar with SAP.

Situation:
When a LAM changes, markers are sometimes removed. These markers are not automatically removed from the LAM in SAP; they are only marked (with the prefix DEL). I have to manually edit the LAM in SAP using transaction IK82 and remove these markers from the LAM.

 

Problem:
If a marker (or the SAP object the marker refers to) has a where used list, I must first edit that where used list — the reference to the marker must be removed there. Only then can I remove the marker from the LAM. However, a marker can have 50 of such usage records — processing all usage records manually is an extremely time-consuming task.

Question:
Are there ways in SAP EAM to remove these markers from a LAM easily? Without having to edit every usage record?

Many thanks

entry.title: Extra Objects getting populated in the Calibration PM order
entry.content:
I need some urgent help analyzing a PM issue. I come from a PTP/MM background and have very limited knowledge of SAP PM, so I’m struggling to identify the root cause.

 

Issue:

 

When a Calibration PM Order is created from a Maintenance Plan where the Reference Object contains both a Functional Location and an Equipment, the generated PM Order contains additional objects.

 

Instead of only the Functional Location and Equipment maintained in the Maintenance Plan, the Objects tab in the PM Order also contains four additional Equipment records. These four Equipment always belong to the different Functional Location, which is unrelated to the Maintenance Plan. Regardless of which Functional Location and Equipment are maintained in the Maintenance Plan, these same four Equipment are always added to the order.

 

However, I noticed that for other Calibration PM Orders created from Maintenance Plans where only the Equipment is maintained in the Reference Object, the generated order behaves correctly. The Objects tab contains only the Equipment and its corresponding Functional Location, with no additional Equipment.

 

I’m trying to understand from where are these extra four Equipment being derived? Any guidance on how to analyze this would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM Order
entry.content:
I need some urgent help analyzing a PM issue. I come from a PTP/MM background and have very limited knowledge of SAP PM, so I’m struggling to identify the root cause.

 

Issue:

 

When a Calibration PM Order is created from a Maintenance Plan where the Reference Object contains both a Functional Location and an Equipment, the generated PM Order contains additional objects.

 

Instead of only the Functional Location and Equipment maintained in the Maintenance Plan, the Objects tab in the PM Order also contains four additional Equipment records. These four Equipment always belong to the different Functional Location, which is unrelated to the Maintenance Plan. Regardless of which Functional Location and Equipment are maintained in the Maintenance Plan, these same four Equipment are always added to the order.

 

However, I noticed that for other Calibration PM Orders created from Maintenance Plans where only the Equipment is maintained in the Reference Object, the generated order behaves correctly. The Objects tab contains only the Equipment and its corresponding Functional Location, with no additional Equipment.

 

I’m trying to understand from where are these extra four Equipment being derived? Any guidance on how to analyze this would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Storage location Mandatory for stock item L at Maintenance order level
entry.content: At level of maintenance order we want Storage location to be mandatory once user select component with specific item category

entry.title: Mass Recording of Inspection Results- Manage Inspection Checklists
entry.content:
Dear Team ,

While performing mass result recording using the F1685A – Record Inspection Results app, the process completes successfully without any errors.

However, when attempting to record results in mass using the F6607 – Manage Inspection Checklists app, the following issues are observed:

  • All the Master Inspection Characteristics (MICs) are defined as quantitative characteristics. No qualitative MICs or code groups are assigned.
  • When selecting all MICs and choosing Record Results, the system displays the following error:
    • "Select inspection characteristics with the same code group."
  • When selecting an individual MIC and choosing Record Results, the system displays the following error:
    • "Could not find suitable code group for inspection characteristics 890000000001 / 1 / 10."

entry.title: deletion task /operation in pm order
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to implement a customization within the PM module of SAP S/4HANA, but I haven't been able to identify the right point to make this change.

I want to remove a specific operation from the task list when a planned maintenance order is being created. How can I achieve this using standard functionality?

Best regards,

entry.title: Hide Tabs on IQ03
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement to hide Tabs (e.g. Sales and Distributions) on IQ03 with Sales Org. level.

Do you know how to achieve this? Is there any Enhancement Points provided by SAP for this?

Thanks in advance.

- p.s. My analysis before:

- SHD0 cannot hide Tabs with Sales Org. level

- IEQM0001 - IEQM0007 looks not suitable for this requirement

Appreciate if you could help.

entry.title: "No personal master record exists for person 0"
entry.content:
Hi Expert,
While click assign to me in perform maintenance job got error "No personal master record exists for person 0"
We didn't have HR (SF)

 

 

BR,

entry.title: Notification on IW31/32 when Position (S) or responsible User is changed (BUS2007)
entry.content:
Is there a workflow that sends a notification when the position (S) is changed in IW31/32? I changed the person responsible to a position (S), but I cannot find a task or rule that filters or resolves this.

My goal is to enter the position as the person responsible in order to distribute the work to my workers; I have two positions. I use a similar workflow for IW22—when I change the position there, the work item disappears from my inbox and lands where it is supposed to go.

However, I can't find anything for IW31/32.

I would appreciate it if anyone has a solution for this.

entry.title: How to compare Work order header saved long text and the current text entered on IW32 while saving.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are comparing the saved Work Order Header Long Text with the current long text entered by the user during IW32 save.

Our current approach is as follows:

- Read the saved long text directly from the STXL table and decompress the cluster data.

- Read the current long text using the READ_TEXT function module during the save process.

- Compare the two texts td lines to determine whether any changes have been made.

 

However, this approach is not working reliably in all scenarios.

 

For example, when a user enters or modifies the long text directly in the OCX long text control, the resulting TDLINE entries differ from those generated when the exact same content is entered through the SAP Text Editor. Although the visible text is identical, the underlying TDLINE structure and line breaks are different, causing the comparison to incorrectly identify the texts as different.

Could you please suggest a robust and reliable approach that can accurately compare the saved and current Work Order Header Long Text regardless of whether the content was entered using the OCX editor or the SAP Text Editor? We are looking for a solution that handles all formatting variations and provides consistent comparison results across all scenarios.

Thanks

entry.title: Internal & External Procurement in asset management
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I have two case 
1- While selecting Material in Maintenance Order with item category "L" after saving and release order 
system not generate direct PR it create a reservation only and i must Run MRP to generate PR . 
Note: in configuration PR/reservation "Immediately"
Is it applicable to generate PR after release if stock missing of the material ?

2- While selecting Material with item category "N" or Service system required purchasing data as mandatory fields
Like (PurOrg, PurGroup)
Is it applicable to make this purchasing data optional ?
Note:
 i tried make data "Blank"  here "Create Default Value Profiles for External Procurement" and assigned it to TL but still get error 
"Purchasing data incomplete" Must enter " Purchasing Org. and Purchasing group"

BR,

entry.title: How to Trace Planned Order Number from Refurbishment Order Created in MD05?
entry.content:
When a planned order is converted into a refurbishment order (PM04) using MD05, the linkage is visible from the MRP side (e.g., in MD04/MD05).

However, is there a standard way to determine the source planned order number from the resulting refurbishment order? If so, could you please advise on the relevant table(s) or field relationships that can be used to establish this linkage?

entry.title: I updated Partners in Master data , But it not shows in work orders
entry.content:
I updated Partners in Master data , But it not shows in work orders

 

For Example, Maintainance responsible person changed in Master data but not shows in work orders
